visualizerParams.title: Digit Factorials
visualizerParams.description: Find the sum of all numbers which are equal to the sum of the factorial of their digits.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
from math import factorial

# Pre-calculate factorials for performance
DIGIT_FACTORIAL = {str(d): factorial(d) for d in range(10)}

def solution() -> int:
    """
    Finds the sum of all numbers equal to the sum of the factorial of their digits.
    Excluded: 1! = 1 and 2! = 2.
    """
    # Upper bound calculation:
    # 9! = 362,880. 7 * 9! = 2,540,160. 
    # A 7-digit number can't exceed this sum by much, so 2.5M is a safe limit.
    grand_total = 0
    for i in range(10, 2540161):
        digit_fact_sum = sum(DIGIT_FACTORIAL[d] for d in str(i))
        if i == digit_fact_sum:
            grand_total += i
    return grand_total


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"Sum of digit factorials: {solution()}")

    grand_total = 0
